div.p: " When it comes again to say the same words, well,
That boy had enemy but his enemy died
[ He was an orphan boy living with
After he died, he used to go hunting in
He had 3 big dogs and a puppy.
They followed him when he went into the woods.
His uncle was sick all the time, I guess consumption
The Puppy that Saved his Master
tiВҙЕЎuhkГ¬ЕЎ lГЁhpК”uwГ nК”ahaВҙ = the door doesn't want to stay shut
kaВҙtan` tiВҙЕЎuhkiЕЎВҙ = where is the door?
kГ ta pro-form taВҙwiЕЎkahДҚ К”Г ran`

tr.td: he was watching him (the old man)
he was watching him (the old man)

div.p: saВҙhkЙ”tЛҮ haВҙtikan again adv wГ hawГӨВҙni.

tr.td: caught up to and passed his master
caught up to and passed his master

div.p: he ask himself " where did he go ?"

tr.td: the hollow place in the tree

div.p: mi, do, do, do, do, do re

tr.td: the hollow place in the tree
do do re, so 2 or 3 times
do do re, so 2 or 3 times

div.p: iВҙriЕЎ ЕЎГ№rukani = I blow my nose
How Flamant Came to be Bald-Headed
At night he turns min - in the daytime
he turn " flamant " - that's why he wanted
to get home before daylight - not said, " but understood this way "
kГ ta pro-form tГ hkapГ№rihДҚ К”Г raВҙ v.chameleon
taВҙДҚekipГ kak К”upГІkaniВҙ = I see the grasshopper
koВҙsuhkК”Г riyaВҙ = poss. crab (has big "fect" in front)
You wet your finger on your tongue + place in on the
bitten area - hold your finger there while you say the Lord's
When you through, put the palm of your hand
Then wet your finger again + make a cross
If it don't cure, take a piece of strong
tobacco and put a little garlic in it + mash it good + make a kind of
plaster + put it on the sore.
then you say Еҫam Еҫam Еҫam ЕҫarГӯ (repeated 4 times)
Don't have to use both treatments.
use the first, or, as a matter of fact, either one.
To cure snake or spider bite
use a certain plant of the mint family - prob.
Pick the leaves the and mash it good - mash a piece of tobacco (like black Grauger)
or a piece of garlic in with the leaves.
- put this on n the sore - leave it on n all
night - the next morning put on another.
The " charm " used in connection with this treatment is a secret.
You wet your finger on your tongue and
place it on the bitten area - hold your finger there
while you say the Lord's prayer - then draw a
circle around the affected area with your finger, then
The charm words are written on a piece of
by the man who taught him
the cure - and now given by S.
(ДҚГ rinaВҙ) - if you do, it'll make a
nГ kawГ ДҚi hipuВҙ = War dance
When they danced the War Dance,
they had a knife, a tomahawk,
They need to take two pieces
of rock and rub them together to make
pГ№na rГ hpahkК”iti = we're playing Ind. ball-game.
(the pole used in ball game was
nailed on top was about 3 ft long,
They used to bet on n the outcome
could make a bet (" Just like
sidi that won got the money.
Never used dice or cards here.
He bet all his people on a
Ball game (1) - Every time your ball hits
They dance at night - play one game
at noon - then they play another game
They play + dance that way sometimes for a
close together - just far enough apart
for a ball to pass through.
they drove two more posts about 3 or 4
acres away - in other words they
didn't touch the ground, when just
were an v.chameleon equal number on n each side.
doesn't know whether there were ever
any names for the two sides.
The Tunica played a game with
so the Choctaw made a plot to
steal the " bets " of both sides.
say they were strong runners, and
some were on n horseback, so they
succeeded in the theft and they
would make a speech before they
them how to get along with people,
to be honest; how they got to behave (got to do)
Sometimes in old times, white men
used to come dance with pretty
Indian girls -- they'd give them a
dollar each time they danced a " set."
drink when they were having a dance --
but they wouldn't bother the young people
at all -- they'd go off and drink
young people wouldn't drink -- they would
They used to have a big feast every
year -- eating their Indian corn, they
call it -- that would last about
Every day they had a big
there was a man & a girl standing
there to invite people to eat.
The white people used to come to eat, too.
One of them old priests he didn't miss --
he come every year -- he knew the
time -- the Indians would come after him.
The ta'wakak got to make a speech --
He made the same kind of speech
hard, then, like now -- it was easy to
live -- and people had plenty of that time.
They used to raise dogs, garden, watermelon,
and so had lots to eat.
They made the Indian corn with
a kind of soup with the beef meat.
Some like to just boil the whole ears
of corn and eat it that way.
In making the soup they cut off the
soft corn kernels and made it into a soup.
They used to make a bread with
the soft corn -- mash up the kernels
and then put it in the green corn
eat it until you smother -- it's so good."
" Talke about something good -- it's better
than them cake they make now."
taВҙkЙ”ramaЕЎakaВҙtakГ lК”uranЛҮ = Where is the glass?
taВҙstК”ГӨkuЛҮ taВҙhkari kГ¬ДҚu kГ lК”uraВҙ = The horse is in the barn.
taВҙstК”ГӨhДҚЛҮ taВҙhkari kГ¬ДҚu kГ lК”ГӨraВҙ = The mare is in the barn.
taВҙyaruhkikЛҮ taВҙhkЙ”ra hГ luht К”uraВҙ = The ax is under the table.
tГЁti hДҒyiht К”ДҒniВҙ = I'm going on the road.
hГЁkina pГ nu К”Г mК”iwГ naВҙ = I'm going far.
hikina pan К”Г mК”iwГ naВҙ = I'm going far.
taВҙrku kiДҚ К”aВҙmК”ГӨkiniВҙ = I wait in the woods.
taВҙrku kiДҚ hopiВҙК”ГӨhkiniВҙ = I come out of the woods.
taВҙrku kiДҚ К”Г mК”iwГ naВҙ = I wait to go into the woods.
taВҙrku the woods n kiДҚ post hopiВҙК”iwГ naВҙ
К”iВҙma nГ¬sa yГ kК”ahkiniВҙ = I just came.
taВҙri kiДҚ К”aВҙmК”uhkiВҙ = He went in the house.
taВҙri kiДҚ hopiВҙК”uhkiВҙ = He came out.
taВҙri kiДҚ К”Г kК”uwГ naВҙ = He wants to go into the house.
taВҙri kiДҚ(h)opiВҙК”uwГ naВҙ = He wants to get out.
taВҙri kiДҚ К”Г№naВҙ = He's sitting in the house
taВҙri kiДҚ hГ rakuВҙ = He's singing in the house.
taВҙriЕЎ hГІn'Й”МҖniВҙ = I'm going down to the house.
kaВҙК”aЕЎ yГ kК”akК”ia(hДҚ)ЛҮ = When you coming back
К”ГЁrusК”ahaВҙ seВҙhili miВҙhta day after tomorrow
kГ ta tГ wohkuhДҚЛҮ = where is the hat
kГ ta tГ wohkuhДҚ К”Г raЛҮ = where is the hat lying
toВҙnikЛҮ К”Г№riЕЎ К”Г mК”uhki = the man went home
taВҙri kaВҙhi haВҙyihta sГ rakuЛҮ = he's praying on the floor
taВҙsakuЛҮ taВҙri kiВҙДҚu ЕЎГ¬mikuВҙ = The dog is playing in the house
taВҙsakЛҮ taВҙri kiВҙДҚu ЕЎГ¬mikuВҙ = The dog is playing in the house
tГ Вҙsa hГ yihta pГ¬tК”uhki = he steppd on the dog
tГ sak К”ukaВҙnК”uhkiВҙ = he stepped over the dog
toВҙnikЛҮ taВҙsaku sГ pК”uhki = The man whipped the dog
hiВҙnahkК”Й”hЙ” yГ К”uhki = he/she didn't do that way
hiВҙnahkК”Й”hЙ” yГ К”aki = g he/she didn't do that way
hiВҙnahkutanЛҮ = it might be that away?
yaВҙkanikК”Г hДҚatanЛҮ = g it might be I'll come?
yГ kК”ikК”Г hДҚatanЛҮ = it might be you'll come?
yГ kК”akК”Г hДҚataЛҮ = it might be you'll come (statement)
kДҒВҙf К”iwanaЛҮ = I want some coffee
taВҙsa rЙ”МҖhpaniВҙ = he's close to the dog
(the only way you could say it)
hiВҙhДҚinЛҮ hЙ”МҖsan К”Г№raВҙ = Right there's a vine
tГ sak К”uhГ¬yahkuВҙ = He's approching (approaches) the dog
tГ sak К”uhГ¬ВҙyanК”Г hkiniВҙ = I woke the dog up
tГ n kГ hi hГ yihta hГ¬nakaniВҙ = I'm marking on the floor
kaВҙta tГ rikГ hihДҚК” = where's the floor?
kaВҙta tГ rikГ hihДҚ К”Г raВҙ = where the floor lying?
kaВҙtanЛҮ К”iВҙrahДҚ = where is my tobacco
kaВҙtanЛҮ К”iВҙyaruhkiЛҮ = where is my ax?
kaВҙtaЛҮ К”iВҙyaruhkihДҚ К”Г ranЛҮ = where is my ax?
kaВҙtaЛҮ К”iВҙyaruhkik К”Г№ran = where is my ax?
You could use either one " them words "
kaВҙta pro-form К”iВҙlЙ”hkОұhДҚ К”Г ranЛҮ v.chameleon
laВҙhontoh = early in the morning ("soon") (about daybreak)
К”aВҙyeihГӨ tГІhК”atahДҚ` = late in the afternoon
taВҙwiЕЎ kГ¬ДҚu mГ№ДҚК”uhki' = he "plunge" into the water
taВҙwiЕЎ kiДҚu mГ№ДҚК”Й”ki = she "plunge" into the water
taВҙri kГ hi hГ yihtОұ kГ lК”ГӨraВҙ = she's standing on the porch
dГІ, dГІ, dГІ, dГІ, dГІ, dГІ, Вҙre
tГ ri kГ hi hГ yiht`
ti, ti, ti, ti, ti, ti
taВҙrku kГ¬ДҚu yГ kК”ihki = He came into the woods
taВҙrku kiДҚu yГ№kК”uhkiВҙ = he around in the woods
lГўВҙhanЛҶ tГ rku n kiДҚ post К”Г mК”uhkiВҙ
tГ ruk kГ¬ДҚu lГ hon К”aВҙmК”uhkiВҙ
lГўtihДҚ` tГ rku n kiДҚ post К”Г mК”uhkiВҙ
taВҙrku the woods n kiДҚ post К”Г mК”uhkiВҙ
kaВҙtОұ tОұ`ЕЎihkalikбөҳ = where is the rock?
kaВҙta tГ ЕЎihkalik К”Г№ranЛҮ = where is the rock lying?
do, ti, do, ti, ti, ti, tiЛҮ, do
do, ti, do, ti, doЛҮ re
taВҙhalta pГ tК”uhkiВҙ = He fell on the ground
taВҙri kГ hi hiyihta paВҙtК”uhkiВҙ = he fell on the floor
taВҙnГӨhtal hГ yoht К”Г№nГ = He's sitting on the bed
taВҙnarak К”Г№waВҙ = the snake is (crawling) going
taВҙnaraku lГ¬sukК”Г№waВҙ = the snake is crawling ("slipping on the ground")
kaВҙta pro-form taВҙwiЕЎГӨnahДҚ К”Г ranЛҮ v.chameleon
pГ rinГӨhkК”Г№nihki = they been doing that
haВҙtikan hГӨВҙntohk К”Г¬wanaВҙ = a little bit more I want
К”Г¬manЛҮ К”Г mariВҙ = I got enough
pГ rК”uhkaВҙ = poss he went off
rГӨ`yakuВҙ = a man, when he got lost
wГІlК”iwГ naВҙ = I want to urinate
pГ rinГӨ`hkakuВҙ = a woman and a man go together
toВҙstohok К”upГ¬rК”aki = a child is born
toВҙstohok nis К”upГ¬ranehki = a child is just born
(there's a tea the woman takes -- but S
doesn't know what it is -- Frances Picoter (pГ¬kotГ©) is a midwife
haВҙЕЎtahahk К”Г hkiri = I'm still living
tiВҙhДҚinЛҮ hiВҙЕЎtahahk К”aВҙkinЛҮ = she still living?
siВҙnimanЛҮ hiВҙЕЎtaha lГўkanЛҮ = they still living (females or animals)
pГ№na rГ hpakataВҙ = they playing racquet ball
Most all them Indians could talk that way.
but it's nearly like Choctaw -- everybody
can speak it -- it's understood by
" They tell me they used to make
persimmon breadk, but I never see that."
They used to break up hickory nuts --
and pick out the kernels and make
kaВҙti mД…`ЕЎ nГ yaЛҮ = where you going
pГ skaВҙ inobana = I want some bread
maВҙК”anЛҮ sГ k К”iwГ naВҙ = I want to eat
The К”Г№ЕЎpi used to talk Mobilian.
haВҙluhta lГ¬kakГ tiВҙ = it's shining down
If you put one of the beans in
The root is a medicine, all right, but
Devil's shoe string -- that's good for a baby.
doesn't know just how it is fixed up,
but he thinks a tea is made from the
root and stalk & leaves -- it's given to a
long a period they give it to the baby, but
it may be for a whole week.
Also if " you have a bad case in the
Court House " and follow this procedure)
They used this to make mattress with.
(They buy it now at $ 2. 50 per 100 lbs.)
doesn't know of any other use made of it by
hide for the mattress covering -- we make
it now with cloth, " but they was making
says the women used to make
At first he didn't know what
part of the mulberry they used, but
then he remembered they used the
He thinks they boil it with sweet milk to
is too bitter -- they couldn't eat that.
" You know what will cure chill (s) quick
Take nine grains of black pepper
and boil it (in about a pint of water) till it reduces
This is good for chills & fever.
This is what Indian doctors used.
tell you drink strong coffee for fever.
Indian doctors did not use this remedy.
says there is a root known as
this root and keep it in your pocket.
When you're going to go away from home,
you take a little bite (break off a little piece)
and chew it and then you " blow " it
to " the four corners " (the four directions),
and while you are blowing it you
make a wish, and while you are
away from home, nothing ain't going to bother you.
kГ ta tГ haranДҚЛҮ = where's the fiddle
doesn't know for what kind of
sickness nor how they fixed it
They used the root of the
It's not good to use this alone,
but they mixed it with the
says " yes ", they used to have the
fast for four days before the
day of the Green Corn Dance.
Then the morning after the four
everybody got to go -- old and young).
You can't taste any kind of
grub, not even a cup of coffee,
before eating the green corn at
they made soup, boiled it, fried
that they take a pleasure, you know."
After dark they started to dance.
In the afternoon they play racquet.
After they dance the Round Dance, if
they feel like it they can have
drink a medicine when they had the
that some drank a medicine to
throw up the bulk of the stomach
was after they ate that they took this
used to root of this tree to make
boiled it -- they got " such a measure "
drink too much -- it might be
poison if you drink too much.
Alligator tree leaves are very astringent -- S M R
Yields a certain amount of tannin -- makes it
rГ№rЙ”watГІhkuВҙ = small ep. of white hickory, has small nuts
When the cut hickory to make
long -- it's so small you can't
tell what kind of hickory it is.
They burn this tree to get the
ДҚГ№hkiyГ№ru = S thinks that it looks like the Sand-Jack Oak
They grow in low places -- don't
bear big acorns -- they're small, small.
They used to make bows with
thinks they, the French, make a tea with
and fry it in fresh lard to make a salve.
You take the leaves of this in the
chicken gumbo, or most any kind
hinahku lГ puВҙ = it's good that way
the gum from the sweet gum tree.
But it's not good to chew it too much --
says you find plenty of them
mayhav on a big prairie back of
rГ¬hkukГ yiВҙ = "yellow tree," a translation from English
Cut it from poles to put in
light, light -- we take that to float
like (" just like ") sweet gum tree leaves)
The flowers of the linden are
dry the flowers (in the sun or
put it in something, and it dry
boil whisky they use a little
bit of the linden flowers boiled
you take a little of that liquid --
do, do, do, do, do, do, mГ®so (or) re
tГ hki yГ ЕЎa yГ katiЛҮ = rheumatism
wГ naniВҙ = I got fever, I'm burning
The Water Ash looks more like
White Ash looks more like what
might be called К”Й”МҖЕЎka yГ ЕЎaВҙ
The root of the black haw (l'alisier)
is used for " the bad disease "
dig the root and boil it in about
of water, and you boil it till
drink it as long as you're sick, 3 or 4 wks.,
fingers in a cup or glass per dose).
Used as a tonic in S.
Sometimes call it nГ№hДҚiyГ ЕЎiВҙ adj.
Bertha DesHotel } -- grandmother (mother's mother) half Tun.
father's daddy -- half Biloxi & half white
Wild cherry and mayapple root were boiled together as well
for chills or fever in Tex.
His mother was S's aunt -- she
was Tunica -- she died 3 or 4 yrs.
The old man (S's father's father) had
The oldest boy was S's father --
was Celina, and the other was
up he went away, and they don't
(dead now, lived at Burkie) had 2 daughters and
taВҙriЕЎ hoВҙnК”aВҙmК”iВҙwana = I want to go down home
К”uВҙhaВҙyihДҚi kaВҙЕЎi pГІwikК”ahaЛҮ = He was so old he couldn't see
In singing the story song sung by
this animal, S says " they must
have had some kind of animals we
К”uЕЎraka pГ nuВҙ lЙ”МҖtawiЕЎtukК”Й”hЙ”ЛҮ = He was so frightened, he couldn't run
said first : tiВҙhДҚinЛҮ pro К”Й”МҖpatahaВҙ adj
wiВҙherihДҚ haВҙwan yaВҙkan` = I want to borrow your pirogue
К”iВҙherihДҚ wihaВҙwihtanikК”Г hДҚaВҙ = I'll lend you my boat
seВҙhita sГ ДҚiwikК”ahДҚaВҙ = It's going to rain tomorrow
К”Й”МҖpatahahДҚi kaВҙЕЎi`pГ¬tawiЕЎtukК”Й”hЙ”ЛҮ = he's so weak he can't run
You dig the root and put in
cold water, leave it in the water
as long as you use it.
it in the water, and the next day
fruit in two and put it on the
fire or on the stone -- and leave
it till you think it's cooked, and
then you put iton a horse's
it on one day, and then the
next day put on a fresh plaster --
says he's heard " a fine little song "
sung by men or women to entertain children
There were also lullabies, sung only
the root in about ВҪ gal.
and let it boil down to about 1 qt.
3 times a day (about 2 fingers or a
have to drink it for one or two
You use this when you can't make
says there is a kind of plant
from which one makes a tea for the flu.
and boil it in about a qt of water
and let it boil down to about a pint.
Stir in sugar and drink it.
You can drink it most any time
doesn't know the name, but they had one.
There is a plant having leaves
boil the root & the whole stalk,
leaves and all, wash it clean,
and boil it in about ВҪ a gal.
water, and let it boil down
Take it 3 x a day before meals.
whether they take it every month or not --
Rabbit Clover -- " the rabbit eat dat ".
К”ГІnilГ№pihiraniВҙ = Indian Pink (flower to the right)
pick that stalk and let it dry, and
mix it with the shot to shoot spirits with
Pole Cone-Flower is a medicine, too,
К”Г ЕЎuhtК”ГӨ wipГІnikК”Г hДҚaВҙ = I'll see you (m.) Sunday
К”ГЁyК”ahkihta = my back of the arm
К”ГЁyutГ pahkaВҙ = bracelet worn on the upper arm
К”ГЁyutГ palaВҙ = bracelet worn on the lower arm
К”ГЁyutГ muriВҙ = bracelet worn on the lower arm
tГ pahДҚuВҙ = ornamental round pieces made of silver, gold
К”ГЁrutГ pahДҚuВҙ = necklace of silver or gold pieces
К”ГЁrusa pГ nuВҙ = I know very well
К”ГЁtiВҙ = my [parents], prob. also "ancestors," relatives, kin; my friend
kaВҙtan` К”ГЁtiВҙ = where are my relatives?
kaВҙtan` К”ГЁtihДҚВҙ = where are my relatives?
lower than o; Й” В· lower than Й”
tГ mihku = something you wear on your head, like a handkerchief
К”ГЁsintГ pahka' = silver or gold ornaments to wear on the head
tГ pahka' = silver or gold ornaments to wear on the head
" They tell me they had an Ind.
on the other side (south) of Opelousas, La.
" They tell me they was right smart (were a good many) several
К”ГЁtikumГ ЕЎiВҙ = sounds good for mother-in-law
doesn't know " father-in-law ", might be above
kaВҙtan` pro-form К”eВҙsin n К”Г raВҙ v.chameleon
re, re, do, do (3 or 4 times)
kaВҙtan` pro-form К”ГЁsintГ lu n [ hДҚ ] '
re, do, do, do, do, re
taВҙstК”ГӨk К”ГЁhtiniВҙ = I own a horse
wГ hkuhkК”utaВҙ = he's putting on breechcloth, getting on drawers
When somebody pinches you without your
К”ГЁhpК”iraВҙ = my shoulder, back of the shoulder (shoulder-blade)
was small, he used to hear
tГЁwal` yГ kК”akiЛҶ (-К”a ki) = my mother, you come quickly
К”ГӨ`lawГӨ`ДҚahЙ”МҖhkaВҙ = the hole of the ear
К”ГЁhkuВҙ = my child (little child or baby) (used by a man or a woman)
К”ГӨ`ntК”ДҒВҙ = my grand-uncle, grandfather, parent, "my old parents"
They got a word for father's sister,
The old people used to have the image of
the bone of foot or the hand of a person.
The foot and part of the
leg to just above the ankle.
The old Indians used to be able to
just look at a person and tell him
They used to know when they
used to use some kind of a root
kaВҙta` К”ГӨ`lakaЕЎtГ ЕЎihpuВҙ = where's the hair-pin
kaВҙta tГ К”Й”saВҙ = where is the tail
kaВҙta pro-form tГ К”Й”sahДҚ n К”Г raВҙ v.chameleon
kaВҙta pro-form К”ГӨВҙЕЎkahДҚ n К”Г raВҙ v.chameleon
К”Й”МҖЕЎkalГ hpiДҚГӨ`raВҙ = boots that tie all the way up
do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do,
mi, re, mi, re, mi, re
К”ГӨ`ЕЎkarГ pun timГ№rakК”Г raniВҙ = I'm putting on my stockings
К”ГӨ`ЕЎkalГ hpi laВҙhpihkК”Г raniВҙ = I'm putting on my shoes
К”Г¬ma yГ ЕЎ К”iyГ К”iki = you hurt me
К”Г¬ma yГ ЕЎ К”iyГ К”Й”nta = they hurt me
yГ ЕЎ К”iyakati = it hurts me
yГІyan rГ¬hki yГ kaniВҙ = I'm doing hard; I'm working hard
yГІyan rЙ”МҖma yГ kaniВҙ = I'm doing heavy work
yГІyan n rГ ЕЎtК”ГӨ adv yГ kaniВҙ
yГІyan lГ pu yГ hkК”araniВҙ = I'm working well
kГ ta tГ 'К”Й”htarЛҮ = where's the wing
kГ ta pro-form tГ К”Й”htarihДҚ n К”Г ranЛҮ v.chameleon
К”ГЁsin yГ ЕЎa yГ katiВҙ = my head aches
kaВҙta taВҙyi`' = where's the wild potatoes
kГ Вҙta pro-form taМҚyihДҚ n К”Г ran`
yГ К”urГ pК”ahkiniВҙ = I killed a deer
tГ ya hГ yiЕЎiВҙ = on the deer
tГ yak К”Г№raВҙ = the deer lies
ya pГІkaniВҙ = I see a deer (fast)
yГ pГІkaniВҙ = I see a deer (slow)
К”ГІni yГ waВҙ = a fast person
tГ nuhДҚi ЕЎЙ”МҖwК”aki = she's with child
Examples of voiceless u after k or hk.
tisaВҙsЗҗn , tiВҙsasЗҗn n, hoВҙtЗ”n v.I, sinwaВҙlikatЙӣВҙnГ¬ v.I.
" kaВҙЕЎkutЗ’hk quant, hЙӣВҙrК”ikК”i v.I laВҙpГ№hДҚ adj,"
ЕЎiВҙhpunК”araВҙ v.II would mean " to pierce "
Lento : К”aВҙlЗҺn n, К”iВҙlЗҗn cardnum, maВҙЕЎК”uhkЙӣВҙnГ¬ v.I.
Stress not dropped before interrogative inflection.
[ page numbers 133-134 not present in original notebook ]
wimuВҙrinikК”ahДҚahaВҙ = I'm not going to throw you in
Some Avoyelles, they say, are still there in
that hole, but they are half fish and half people.
When the ocean dried up, they couldn't
get back to the water, so they stay in
This hole is near the Avoyelles
emergence hole, but is not the same one.
There are 2 holes there, one is the Avoyelles
hole, the other the ocean animal hole.

tr.td: a spider species having seven legs
a spider species having seven legs

div.p: Does not know whether this was before
Used to be several tribes by
and the Pascagoula must be friends
to war against the Tunica and been
The Tunica must been " right smart "
In old book, Y heard this.
by old Indian he heard this.
Old man Mingo, a big chief
was close kin to Y's people.
He used to be chief on Bayou Clair.
Y says sm told him Koasati from Alabama.
Y thinks there is a village of Opelousas Indians
15 miles SW " must be " from Catholic Church in Opelousas, La.
Never been there himself but that's what he heard.
Й”МЁ pasЙ”МЁ (Йӣ)stewaВҙ dЙ”МЁtЙ”МЁsЙ”МЁВҙ = I pass by and see you in your blood
Send word to S and he
could cure it without seeing or being near the
Then name your name again and say
the words again and do this seven times
" riВҙhkuДҚehkaВҙlК”uraВҙ the chair stands n К”uВҙkК”itГ n Sit down! v.I."
hiВҙЕЎtahahaВҙrakuВҙ = he sings all the time
has never seen the Sun Dance
though he knows the song (which has
" They tell me they dance it something
26. hinahkЕӯn adv, ЕЎuВҙДҚin v.I, К”uВҙrЗҺ v.chameleon, O.
Pinewood is at a place they call kukoВҙdri, a (or Pawnee)
flag station before you get to Oakdale,
К”uВҙkК”ikК”Зҗ, К”uВҙkК”ikК”ЗҗhДҚ, laВҙpЗ”hДҚ adj, nikuВҙ v.I
К”uВҙwihinyaВҙwЗҗhДҚ tikiВҙhtК”Йӣhkin = he did it to me, + I pinch her
After they plunge, they go back
same thing with corn and bean.
Once a year for corn and once a year for
The men pack wood to make
a fire for women to cook on.
they start a dance good they
dance a week before they stop.
" The Indians fast to eat the bean,
just like they fast to eat the corn."
Before the eat the bean, they serve
Fast for four days before they eat.
They " appoint " a day four days
before they eat in order to fast for
They get up in morning early before
They got a little vessel (dish) and put it
to the head of each grave.
tell me Tunica used to put head of corpse
Then they go in river and plunge
The commander (taВҙwakak n) tell them to plunge
hinyaВҙti conn sic as to stress.
" She made friends with one of them 'varmints' "
laВҙwuhtЙӣВҙpЗҺn n, hopiВҙwЗҗhДҚ v.I, heВҙrikЙ”Вҙni v.I
К”iyuВҙkК”arЗҺhДҚ v.I, hiВҙnahkЗ”n adv, К”aВҙmК”ЙӣkiВҙ v.I.
taВҙnarЗҺk hatoВҙlunaВҙК”uhkЙӣВҙnГ¬ = the snake is coiled up
liВҙsu = to slip along on ground
miВҙnu riВҙsakЙ”ЛіЛіtaВҙ = grey cat, tiger cat
kuВҙwatoВҙlutoh = some kind of gregarious bird
feathers pretty, sounds like their redbird
h-hДҚ conn, К”uВҙwЗҗn pro, " iВҙkЗҗku n, hopiВҙniЕЎtuhkК”Й”hЙ” v.I, К”iniВҙК”uhkiВҙ ,"
maВҙК”ahЗҺn pro, К”iВҙwanaВҙ v.st Also O.
|| or now wants to say : maК”iwaВҙnК”ahaВҙ pro
maВҙК”ЗҺn v.I, К”iВҙwanaВҙ v.st nikЙ”ВҙnГ¬ v.I.
К”uВҙЕЎira n К”aВҙhkihtЗҺn post, piВҙhunК”uhkЙӣВҙni v.II.
tiВҙrasaniВҙ n } usually same syllable weight in penult.
Correct to : (prob no need to correct)
toВҙnisЙӣВҙmЗҺn К”uВҙkК”ЙӣrasЙӣВҙmЗҺn К”uВҙkinaВҙК”arasЙӣВҙmЗҺn hiВҙpunaВҙК”arahaВҙnГ¬ = the people who were sitting were not dancing
toВҙnisЙӣВҙm К”uВҙkК”ЙӣrahaВҙnГ¬ = there were no people
112. Correct hareВҙpowЗҗhДҚ v.I to haВҙrepoВҙwЗҗhДҚ v.I
Some confusion between cough, throw up, pant and the like.
Gives : kuВҙhukuВҙ v.T.tr = he's coughing (instead of К”uВҙhu v.T.tr as formerly)
Says : К”uВҙhukuВҙ v.I = he's vomiting (instead of leВҙhu v.I as formerly)
Says К”uВҙhukuВҙ v.I, ruВҙhukuВҙ , and kuВҙhukuВҙ v.I over and over
but has difficulty in distinguishing their meanings.
К”iroВҙkukati v.T.tr = mosquito bit me and I itch
It would seem that p is always aspirated
all stops slightly aspirated -- heavily asp.
seВҙhitilaВҙtЗҗhДҚ v.TR.f, К”aВҙrupoВҙnikК”ahaВҙ v.I = I won't dream
" saВҙhukuВҙn adj, luВҙpК”uhkikЕӯn v.I," К”uВҙnikЙ”ВҙnГ¬ v.I p. 184
Used to be (1907) about 500 Koasati -- now the old ones have
died out & many of the young ones have gone to Texas.
Some Indians living close to St.
man I saw at Frances' house saw them coming into
yanquipin, or wata chinquipin, or monoca (mОұnaВҙkОұ) nut -- ninoc
? is there any connection between the above and yucca
Has a tough fiber and is used for ropes and cords --
used to tie up meat to smoke it.
resembling radish, but when you boil
it the water gets red, red.
in September, it's dried up & the stalk
Use leaves & bark of Magnolia tree
to make a yellow dy for cane.
This was used over on the Mississippi R.,
also on the Washita -- perhaps used at
Plants to be identified by C.
It's poison if you drink it.
Boil & drink the roots is poison.
The Puppy That Saved His Master... 3
How the Flamant got his Bald head... 25
The Drying up of the ocean... 137
war with the Pascagoula & the Natchez... 139
